description_long: This object is a double-ended weapon resembling a staff with hooked blades at both ends. The central section is wrapped or reinforced with a metallic or possibly wooden grip, featuring orange and red accents that may serve as decorative or functional bands. The blades are dark, metallic, and slightly curved, each ending in a sharp hook, suggesting a design for slashing or hooking actions. The central grip area appears to be reinforced and possibly blood-stained, indicating use or a decorative motif. The overall construction is symmetrical, with both ends mirroring each other. The object has a rugged, utilitarian appearance, suitable for close combat or display as an exotic weapon.
description: A double-ended staff weapon with hooked, curved blades and a reinforced, decorated central grip featuring orange and red accents.
description_view_0: The full length of the staff is visible, showing both hooked blades and the central grip with orange and red details.
description_view_1: A side profile showing the thinness of the object and the layered construction of the central grip.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, displaying the symmetrical hooked blades and the decorated central grip.
description_view_3: A side profile from the opposite direction, again highlighting the slim profile and central grip details.
attribute_handle_design: straight, reinforced central grip with orange and red decorative bands
attribute_ornamentation: orange and red decorative bands, possible blood-stain motif
